name: Pasta Aglio e Olio
description: The Roman classic — garlic, chili flakes, olive oil, parmesan. Embarrassingly simple, unreasonably good.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in spiced tomato sauce with cumin and paprika. Serve with crusty bread.

name: Teriyaki Zalm met Rijst
description: Pan-fried salmon with sweet-salty teriyaki glaze. Rice and steamed broccoli on the side.

name: Rode Linzensoep
description: Creamy red lentil soup with cumin and lemon. One pot, zero effort, maximum comfort.

name: Bibimbap
description: Rice bowl with sautéed vegetables, a fried egg, and gochujang sauce. Colourful and satisfying.

name: Pittige Garnalen Pasta
description: Prawns in garlic-chili-tomato sauce tossed with linguine. Weeknight fancy in 20 minutes.

name: Stamppot Boerenkool
description: The ultimate Dutch winter comfort. Mashed potatoes with kale, served with rookworst and mustard.

name: Tikka Masala
description: Chicken in creamy, mildly spiced tomato-yoghurt sauce. Aromatic but not too spicy. With naan or rice.

name: Caprese Salade met Bruschetta
description: Fresh mozzarella, tomato, basil, balsamic. With garlic bruschetta. Summer on a plate.

name: Pad Thai
description: Stir-fried rice noodles with tofu, peanuts, bean sprouts, and tamarind sauce. Sweet-sour-salty perfection.

name: Burrito Bowl
description: Rice, black beans, corn, avocado, salsa, cheese, sour cream. Build-your-own-bowl style.

name: Erwtensoep
description: Thick Dutch split pea soup with smoked sausage and rye bread. Weekend project, feeds you for days.

name: Couscous met Geroosterde Groenten
description: Fluffy couscous with oven-roasted peppers, courgette, and aubergine. Feta and mint on top.

name: Udon Noodle Soup
description: Thick udon noodles in warm dashi broth with mushrooms and soft-boiled egg. Cozy in a bowl.

name: Gehaktballen in Tomatensaus
description: Homemade meatballs simmered in rich tomato-basil sauce. With spaghetti or crusty bread.

name: Groene Curry
description: Quick green curry with coconut milk, sugar snaps, and tofu. Aromatic and creamy. Serve over jasmine rice.

name: Quiche Lorraine
description: Buttery pastry shell filled with bacon, gruyère, and egg custard. Serve with green salad.

name: Falafel Wraps
description: Crispy falafel in warm pita with hummus, pickled onion, and tahini sauce. Better than takeaway.

name: Risotto ai Funghi
description: Creamy mushroom risotto with parmigiano. Requires stirring and patience. Worth it.

name: Tostada's met Zwarte Bonen
description: Crispy tortillas topped with mashed black beans, avocado, salsa, and cheese. 15-minute dinner.

name: Kip Saté met Pindasaus
description: Grilled chicken skewers with homemade peanut sauce. With rice, kroepoek, and atjar.

name: Pasta Pesto met Mozzarella
description: Penne with green pesto, cherry tomatoes, and torn mozzarella. The lazy genius dinner.

name: Nasi Goreng
description: Dutch-Indonesian fried rice with ketjap, vegetables, and a fried egg on top. Use leftover rice.

name: Makreel met Bietensalade
description: Smoked mackerel on a bed of roasted beet, apple, and horseradish cream. Light but filling.

name: Pannenkoeken
description: Dutch pancakes. Savoury with cheese and ham, sweet with stroop and poedersuiker. Or both. No judgement.

name: Dahl met Naanbrood
description: Spiced yellow lentil stew with ginger, turmeric, and tomato. Creamy, warming, protein-rich.

name: Shoarma Bowl
description: Seasoned chicken strips with hummus, cucumber, tomato, and garlic sauce over rice. Deconstructed kebab.

name: Pompoensoep
description: Roasted pumpkin soup with ginger and coconut milk. Serve with pumpkin seed oil and sourdough.

name: Tagliatelle Bolognese
description: A proper slow-cooked ragù. Weekend effort that rewards patience. Freeze half for next week.

name: Gevulde Paprika's
description: Bell peppers stuffed with rice, feta, sun-dried tomatoes, and herbs. Baked until tender.

name: Fish Tacos
description: Crispy white fish in soft tortillas with mango salsa, red cabbage slaw, and lime crema.

name: Bami Goreng
description: Stir-fried egg noodles with vegetables and ketjap manis. The nasi goreng's noodle sibling.

name: Croque Monsieur
description: The French grilled cheese: ham, gruyère, béchamel. Serve with a simple green salad.

name: Gnocchi met Salie Boter
description: Pan-fried gnocchi in brown butter with crispy sage leaves and parmesan. Ridiculously easy, ridiculously good.

name: Vietnamese Pho
description: Aromatic beef broth with rice noodles, herbs, and lime. The simplified weeknight version.

name: Flammkuchen
description: Ultra-thin crispy base with crème fraîche, onions, and bacon. The Alsatian pizza. Beer required.

name: Shakshuka Verde
description: Green shakshuka with spinach, leek, and feta instead of the tomato version. Fresh and light.

name: Pulled Jackfruit Sandwich
description: Shredded jackfruit in BBQ sauce on brioche buns with coleslaw. Surprisingly meaty for a plant.

name: Stamppot Hutspot
description: Mashed potatoes with carrots and onions. Classic Dutch comfort with a klapstukje or braadworst.

name: Miso Aubergine met Rijst
description: Oven-roasted aubergine with sweet miso glaze. With steamed rice and quick-pickled cucumber.

name: Chicken Over Rice
description: NYC halal cart-style. Spiced chicken strips on yellow rice with a creamy white sauce. Better than standing in line.
